text.title: Prompt Techniques — Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules for advanced prompt engineering in production. Source: OpenAI / Anthropic prompt engineering guides, prompt-injection research, internal regression logs.

rule.statement: Sections of multi-part prompts are wrapped in XML-style tags (e.g., `<document>`, `<question>`, `<format>`). Plain backtick fences are accepted but XML tags are preferred for injection resistance.
rule.rationale: Models attend to tag boundaries strongly; tag-delimited sections survive prompt injection better than ambiguous backticks.

rule.statement: Any prompt generated by meta-prompting is reviewed by a human and stored as a versioned PromptLibrary entry. Auto-promotion of meta-generated prompts is forbidden.
rule.rationale: LLM-generated prompts are model-specific, may not transfer; auto-promotion bypasses regression checks.

rule.statement: Every PromptChain step is idempotent — re-running with the same input produces the same output. Side-effecting steps (DB writes, API calls) live outside the chain.
rule.rationale: Non-idempotent steps make retry semantics dangerous; partial failures corrupt downstream state.

rule.statement: A prompt change is A/B tested against the previous version on the golden set. Promotion to production requires accuracy lift ≥ 0% AND no regression in any tracked metric.
rule.rationale: Untested promotions are the #1 cause of silent quality drops; A/B with a fixed test set blocks them.

rule.statement: PromptLibrary entries live in YAML files committed to the repo. Each entry has `slug`, `version`, `model`, `prompt`, `eval_score`. Hot-fix requires PR, not console edit.
rule.rationale: In-DB prompts evade review and version control; YAML in repo preserves both.

rule.statement: CI runs every PromptLibrary version against the golden set; PR merge is blocked if eval_score drops more than 1% absolute from the baseline.
rule.rationale: Without an automated gate, accuracy decay is detected only by users.
